task: Write a Python function sum_distinct(nums) returning the sum of the distinct values in the list, each value counted once no matter how many times it repeats. An empty list returns 0. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all the given criteria.
trace: The function takes a list `nums` as input and returns the sum of its distinct values. First, we convert the list into a set to eliminate duplicates since sets in Python do not allow duplicate elements. Then, we use the built-in `sum()` function to calculate the total sum of the distinct numbers in the set. In case the list is empty, the function returns 0 as per the given criteria. The named edge case handled here is an empty list input.
